title: FX Brief — 08 Jul 2026
intro:
USD consolidates after prior session losses as markets await key US labor data later this week, with yen weakness persisting above 162 despite intervention concerns.
💪 CURRENCY RANKING
NZD +0.29% — Kiwi leads on risk appetite and commodity strength despite Asian session profit-taking
CHF +0.24% — Safe-haven flows as USD/CHF corrects from overbought levels
CAD +0.16% — Loonie supported by oil stabilization and broad USD softness
AUD -0.15% — Aussie consolidates after strong settlement gains, ranging ahead of RBA guidance
EUR -0.18% — Euro pauses near 1.1420 as traders lock profits from recent rally
GBP -0.30% — Sterling retreats from highs on technical resistance at 1.34 handle
USD -0.10% — Greenback neutral with modest intraday strength but settlement losses persist
JPY -0.13% — Yen remains weakest at 162.30, vulnerable to intervention risk yet unsupported

sections.heading: 📊 FX THEME
sections.content:

💪 CURRENCY RANKING

CAD +0.16% — Loonie supported by oil stabilization and broad USD softness
AUD -0.15% — Aussie consolidates after strong settlement gains, ranging ahead of RBA guidance
EUR -0.18% — Euro pauses near 1.1420 as traders lock profits from recent rally
GBP -0.30% — Sterling retreats from highs on technical resistance at 1.34 handle
USD -0.10% — Greenback neutral with modest intraday strength but settlement losses persist

sections.heading: 🎯 TRADE IDEAS
sections.content:
LONG: NZD/JPY | 92.50 | 92.00 | 93.80 | Kiwi momentum vs persistent yen weakness
LONG: GBP/USD | 1.3340 | 1.3300 | 1.3425 | Dip buying into support with bullish settlement trend
SHORT: USD/JPY | 162.40 | 163.00 | 161.20 | Intervention risk escalates above 162, mean reversion play
SHORT: EUR/CAD | 1.6180 | 1.6240 | 1.6080 | CAD outperformance continues on oil stability

sections.heading: ⚠️ RISKS
sections.content: USD/JPY intervention threat intensifies above 162.00 with BOJ/MOF rhetoric likely. US labor data Thursday-Friday could trigger volatility across all dollar pairs if payrolls disappoint expectations.
